user.title: Recorder Throttle
user.description: Throttles per-entity DB writes (via rec-* labels). Configure the scan for new unthrottled heavy writers here.

data.scan_enabled: Report new heavy writers in Repairs

abort.single_instance_allowed: Already configured — only a single instance is supported.

init.title: Recorder Throttle — settings
init.description: Turn the switch off to never create the Repairs notice. When on, the threshold is the write rate above which an unthrottled entity is reported.

data.scan_enabled: Report new heavy writers in Repairs

confirm.title: Stop reporting new heavy writers?
confirm.description: This turns off the scan for new unthrottled heavy writers, so this notice won't appear again. You can re-enable it anytime under the integration's options.

hook_not_installed.title: Recorder Throttle: hook not installed
hook_not_installed.description: Recorder Throttle could not patch the recorder, so database writes are **not** being throttled (the recorder itself keeps running normally). This usually happens after a Home Assistant update changed the recorder internals — update the integration or check the log.
